machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs.
machinery_genes.note: Deng et al 2004 systematically mutagenized all 41 C. rodentium (ICC168 LEE) genes; escR named as one of the 10 conserved core TTSS apparatus genes. SctR export-gate role from the apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs.
machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctS export-gate role from brief.

machinery_genes.quote: Among the 41 LEE genes, 10 (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, and escF) encode proteins conserved among TTSSs.
machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctT export-gate role from brief.

machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctU autoprotease/switch role from brief.

machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctC outer-membrane secretin role from brief.

machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein role from brief.

machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctV export-apparatus ring role from brief.

machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctN ATPase role from brief.

machinery_genes.note: C. rodentium LEE conserved core gene (Deng et al 2004). SctD inner MS-ring role from brief.

machinery_genes.quote: In this study, we investigated EscF, a protein sharing sequence similarity with MxiH and PrgI. We report that EscF is required for type III protein secretion and EspA filament assembly.
machinery_genes.note: Wilson et al 2001 characterise EscF as the EPEC LEE needle protein (homologue of Shigella MxiH and Salmonella PrgI needle subunits). Same LEE/Esc system as C. rodentium; escF is also one of Deng et al 2004's 10 conserved C. rodentium LEE core genes (PMID 14988506). Strain transfer: structural characterisation in EPEC, gene present and required in C. rodentium LEE.

machinery_genes.role: filamentous surface organelle extending the needle, conduit for translocated proteins
machinery_genes.quote: EspA is a structural protein and the major component of a large transiently expressed filamentous surface organelle that forms a direct link between the bacterium and the host cell
machinery_genes.note: Wilson et al 2001 (EPEC LEE). EspA filament is an extension of the type III secretion needle (apparatus, not effector), per the brief's translocon/tip category. Same LEE system as C. rodentium.

machinery_genes.quote: whereas EspD and EspB seem to form the mature translocation pore
machinery_genes.note: Wilson et al 2001 (EPEC LEE). EspD is a translocator forming the host-membrane pore (apparatus per brief, despite being secreted). Same LEE system as C. rodentium; EspD secretion in C. rodentium documented by Neves et al 2003 (PMID 12654835).

machinery_genes.quote: whereas EspD and EspB seem to form the mature translocation pore
machinery_genes.note: Wilson et al 2001 (EPEC LEE). EspB is a translocator forming the host-membrane pore (apparatus per brief). Same LEE system as C. rodentium.

machinery_genes.quote: 13 other LEE genes were needed for TTS, with 9 (orf2, orf4, orf5, rorf3, rorf8, orf12, orf15, sepQ, and orf29) required for both translocator and effector secretion
machinery_genes.note: Deng et al 2004 name sepQ among C. rodentium LEE genes required for type III secretion of both translocators and effectors. SepQ corresponds to EscQ (SctQ family, cytoplasmic C-ring/sorting platform) per the apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: 4 (orf3, rorf6, orf16, and sepL) affecting translocator secretion preferentially
machinery_genes.note: Deng et al 2004: sepL is required for type III secretion and preferentially affects translocator secretion; the paper describes SepL/Rorf6 as a molecular switch controlling the secretion hierarchy of translocators and effectors. Maps to the SctW gatekeeper family (InvE/MxiC/YopN-TyeA) per the brief. Included as apparatus (secretion-control machinery), not as a transcriptional regulator.

notes: This instance is the C. rodentium ICC168 injectisome (Esc/Sep T3SS) encoded by the locus of enterocyte effacement (LEE) genomic island. The genome paper (Petty et al 2010, PMID 19897651) establishes that ICC168 'exploits a type III secretion system (T3SS) to induce attaching and effacing (A/E) lesions' and that the LEE 'encodes a T3SS and effector proteins'. The instance's three curated effectors (ROD_29641/29651/29661, sys_instance_label prophage_CRP3) are non-LEE-encoded (NLE) effectors carried on a prophage region but translocated by this LEE-encoded apparatus; the prophage carries no T3SS of its own, so the answer-key machinery is the single LEE-encoded Esc/Sep injectisome. Core apparatus from Deng et al 2004 (PMID 14988506), the definitive paper that systematically mutagenized all 41 C. rodentium LEE genes: the 10 conserved esc genes (escR, escS, escT, escU, escC, escJ, escV, escN, escD, escF) plus sepQ (C-ring) and sepL (gatekeeper switch). EscF (needle), EspA (filament), and EspB/EspD (translocation pore) structurally characterised in the EPEC LEE by Wilson et al 2001 (PMID 11696035) and Daniell et al 2001 (PMID 11736997); strain transfer noted because EPEC and C. rodentium share the homologous LEE/Esc system (same Frankel-lab system, and escF is among Deng's 10 conserved C. rodentium genes). Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the translocated effectors (Tir, Map, EspF, EspG, EspH, EspZ, EspS and the NLE/prophage effectors including this instance's ROD_296xx set); the T3SS chaperones CesT, CesD, CesD2, CesF, CesAB (CesD2 documented in C. rodentium by Neves et al 2003, PMID 12654835, cited as apparatus-context but the chaperone itself is excluded); and the LEE regulators Ler, GrlA, GrlR. Lower-confidence / not listed: sepD, sepZ, escI (inner rod), orf16/escP (ruler) and other orf/rorf genes that Deng shows are needed for TTS but for which I could not extract a verbatim sentence naming the individual gene as a named apparatus component (only their collective listing as 'needed for TTS'); these were omitted to respect the quality-over-completeness and naming-quote rules. SepQ is identified with EscQ (SctQ) on the basis of family/role; the verbatim quote uses the locus designation sepQ.
